fumadocsTargets.covers: GET /v1/ad-config endpoint: description, secret-key auth, request examples, and full response schema (enabled, frequency, firstAdOffset, maxPerPage, adTagUrl, adUnitId, skipPolicy, skipDelaySeconds, defaultTargeting, native* fields) generated from openapi.public.json

notes: Get Ad Config fully ported to OpenAPI-generated page; all response fields present in spec example (plus extra sponsored* fields), desc and auth match.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Bulk of the object semantics: placement fields (enabled/firstAdOffset/frequency/maxPerPage) with defaults in a table, skip behavior (skipPolicy/skipDelaySeconds/defaultTargeting) with defaults, ad-source fields (adTagUrl/adUnitId), native ad fields (nativeEnabled/nativeFormatId/nativeAdUnitId/native*Key mapping to videoUrl/headline/ctaText/sponsorName/sponsorLogo), the placement algorithm diagram, the ad_slot feed-shape JSON example, and the 'update other fields while disabled' caveat. Also adds the new sponsored* fields.
Generated GET /v1/ad-config endpoint (intro says default values returned if none saved). Replaces the object page's 'read config' role; secret-key auth shown in OpenAPI security.

Generated PUT /v1/ad-config endpoint whose AdConfigWrite request-body schema enumerates all 18 object fields (enabled, frequency, firstAdOffset, maxPerPage, adTagUrl, adUnitId, skipPolicy, skipDelaySeconds, defaultTargeting, nativeEnabled, nativeFormatId, nativeAdUnitId, native*Key) plus sponsored*; documents sparse-update behavior.

unported.item: Native-ad field DEFAULT values (nativeVideoUrlKey=video_url, nativeHeadlineKey=headline, nativeCtaKey=cta_text, nativeSponsorKey=sponsor_name, nativeSponsorLogoKey=sponsor_logo) are not shown as defaults anywhere; the OpenAPI AdConfigWrite schema carries no defaults/descriptions and the ads guide only names the keys in prose without their default values.

The native-variant ad_slot JSON example (adFormat:"native" with nativeAssetKeys mapping object) is reproduced literally in Mintlify but only described in prose in the ads guide; only the non-native ad_slot JSON is shown verbatim.

notes: Object fields fully covered by ads guide (defaults+semantics) plus generated GET/PUT endpoint schemas; only native-key defaults and native ad_slot JSON example are softened, both low.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire page: PUT /v1/ad-config endpoint, sparse-update behavior (description), all 17 request body params (enabled, frequency, firstAdOffset, maxPerPage, adTagUrl, adUnitId, skipPolicy, skipDelaySeconds, defaultTargeting, nativeEnabled, nativeFormatId, nativeAdUnitId, nativeVideoUrlKey, nativeHeadlineKey, nativeCtaKey, nativeSponsorKey, nativeSponsorLogoKey via AdConfigWrite schema), request examples, and 200 response shape with all data fields + meta.request_id — all OpenAPI-generated

notes: Fully ported to OpenAPI-generated api/ad-configuration/update-ad-configuration; sparse-update note, all params, and response fields all present (schema even adds sponsored* fields).

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire page: how it works, enable forwarding (all 4 SDK snippets), Configuration table, What gets forwarded (event types, batch shape, event envelope), Build your collector, Field reference (viewEnd/swipe/playbackFallback), Aggregating into sessions, Best practices

fumadocsTargets.covers: Full event-type catalog (Engagement, Playback quality, Feed navigation, View lifecycle, Ad lifecycle tables with Type/Description/key event_data fields) at #event-types, plus the custom-event-types Callout, plus the SDK-auto-collection intro behavior.

The event object shape — generated GET /v1/events/explore response example contains every attribute (id, event_type, content_id, user_id, session_id, view_session_id, timestamp, received_at, event_data), and the event_type/content_id/viewer_id filter params.

notes: Event-type catalog fully reproduced in overview#event-types; object fields appear in generated list-events example. POST /v1/events reference intentionally removed (SDK-only).

fumadocsTargets.covers: The entire GET /v1/metrics/{metric} endpoint: path param `metric`, query params `measurement` (sum/avg) and `timeframe[]` (relative/absolute, default 7:days), response envelope (data.value, total_views, timeframe, meta.request_id via OpenAPI example), and request/response code samples. The rich page description enumerates all metric families (views, unique_viewers, completions, watch_time, playing_time, video_startup_time) with their default measurements and units, covering the extra cURL examples (completions over 30 days, avg video_startup_time over 24 hours).

The metric catalog the Mintlify page linked to as 'Metrics Overview' — engagement/QoE/context metric definitions (watchTimeMs, playingTimeMs, videoStartupTimeMs, etc.) that the metric slugs aggregate.

notes: Endpoint fully covered by OpenAPI-generated get-a-metric page; param tables auto-generated, metric list/timeframe/filters semantics in page description; metric catalog also in analytics overview.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire endpoint: GET /v1/metrics/{metric}/timeseries. OpenAPI-generated page. Path param `metric`, query `measurement` (sum/avg, aggregate-only, ignored by count metrics), query `timeframe[]` (relative value:unit / absolute two Unix timestamps, default 7:days), auto granularity (hourly <=24h, daily otherwise), response schema (data array of date/value, timeframe, meta.request_id), and auto-generated request code samples. The 'Examples' variations (hourly completions over 24h, daily watch_time sum over 7d) are param combinations whose behavior is documented in the page description.
Metric slug catalog referenced by the Mintlify link to Metrics Overview (the full list of queryable metric names like views, completions, watch_time).

notes: Direct 1:1 match to OpenAPI-generated get-metric-timeseries page; all params, behavior, and response example present. Param tables auto-generated; hand examples are param variations covered by description.

fumadocsTargets.covers: The endpoint itself (GET /v1/events/explore): query params event_type, content_id, viewer_id, limit (default 50, min 1, max 200), page (default 1); the timeframe[] param (relative value:unit and absolute Unix-timestamp forms, default 7:days) documented in the operation description; the paginated response example with data array (id, event_type, content_id, user_id, session_id, view_session_id, event_data, timestamp, received_at, plus playback_id), pagination object (total, page, limit, total_pages), timeframe, meta.request_id; page-based pagination behavior (newest-first ordering); and time-range filtering.

The #event-types catalog backing the event_type filter (playStart, completion, like, etc. with associated event_data fields); linked from the list-events page description. Also notes custom event types are accepted/stored/returned.

notes: All params, response fields, pagination, and time-range filtering are in the generated list-events page; event-type catalog lives in analytics/overview#event-types. UUID->prefixed-ID examples are intentional.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Core endpoint: count metrics (views, unique_viewers, completions), aggregate metrics (watch_time/sum, playing_time/sum, video_startup_time/avg) with measurement param, and full timeframe spec (relative value:unit, absolute Unix range, default 7:days). Description prose carries all the Mintlify metric semantics; param tables are OpenAPI-generated.
The 'both endpoints accept timeframe[]' framing — confirms the same metric slugs, measurement, and timeframe parameter format/default apply to the time-bucketed metric endpoint.
Conceptual intro: metrics aggregate per-view-session data, viewEnd carries the full viewing-session summary and powers the view and metric endpoints, and analytics endpoints require the secret key.

notes: All metrics-overview content (timeframes, count + aggregate metrics, measurement, secret-key, video-views/viewEnd framing) is fully represented in get-a-metric, timeseries, and overview.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Nearly the entire page: API key types table (publishable vs secret, prefix/header/purpose/security), key formats, publishable & secret key sections, endpoints-by-key-type tables, environment isolation, secret key rotation, key expiration, authentication errors pointer, and all five best-practices accordions.
401 invalid_api_key and 403 forbidden status codes referenced by the auth-errors section (HTTP status codes table).

SDK initialization with the publishable key via ShortKit(apiKey:) — the swift init snippet (apiKey/config/userId) from the Publishable keys section; analogous setup pages (android, web, etc.) cover the other platforms.

POST /v1/content/upload — listed in Mintlify's publishable-key endpoints table; in Fumadocs it is a dual-key (publishable or secret) endpoint, surfaced from the auth page's 'accept either key type' note.

notes: Faithful near-1:1 port. Vendor/impl detail (bcrypt) generalized to 'hashed', SDK-only endpoints (feed/events/identity/captions) intentionally removed from key tables, and rotate-secret-key endpoint replaced with portal flow — all intentional migration changes. No genuine gaps.

fumadocsTargets.covers: The entire endpoint: GET /v1/content/{content_id} operation, path param (id -> content_id), request examples (curl/python/node auto-generated), and the 200 response with all body fields (contentType, uploadStatus, publishStatus, duration, streamingUrl, thumbnailUrl, section, author, tags, customMetadata, captionTracks, images, publishAt, expiresAt, aspectRatio, createdAt, updatedAt). Generated from openapi.public.json.
Semantic descriptions of the ContentItem response fields (title, description, duration, streamingUrl, thumbnailUrl, captionTracks, customMetadata, author, etc.) referenced by the response object.
The documented 404 behavior (content not found / not owned by org) as standard error handling.

notes: Fully ported. get-content.mdx is OpenAPI-generated for GET /v1/content/{content_id}; param, examples, and all response fields present. width/height appear as videoWidth/videoHeight (schema rename, not a gap).

fumadocsTargets.covers: POST /v1/content/import endpoint — OpenAPI-generated page; request params (callbackUrl, items[] with contentType/title/description/tags/customMetadata/section/author/publishAt/expiresAt/generateSubtitles/sourceUrl/externalId, images[] with sourceUrl/altText/position), 201 response with data/summary/queue/meta, max 100 items, idempotency via externalId, 429 quota note
POST /v1/content/import/tiktok — platform import endpoint; urls (video or profile), profile auto-expansion (maxVideosPerProfile), dedup of previously imported, callbackUrl, async jobId response
POST /v1/content/import/instagram — platform import endpoint; urls (Reel or profile), maxReelsPerProfile expansion, dedup, callbackUrl, async jobId response
GET /v1/content/import/status — import queue status, daily quota usage, queued/processing/completed/failed counts, estimated drain time (referenced by import.mdx as the import status endpoint)

Conceptual import narrative: URL import use case (migration/CMS/cloud storage), up to 100 items, same metadata fields as upload, per-item status + summary, individual item failure isolation, externalId idempotency, Platform imports table (tiktok/instagram), auto-populated metadata table (title/description/author/customMetadata for TikTok & Instagram), update-after-import note, content-rights warning, platform-availability caveat

Callback lifecycle: callbackUrl registration, content.imported (processing then ready) payloads incl. playbackId/source/timestamp, content.import_failed payload, 3-attempt retry with 1s then 5s delays, fire-and-forget semantics, 2xx ack, missed-event recovery via GET /v1/content/{id}, callbacks only for video imports (carousels synchronous/already ready)
Video carousels cannot be created via the import endpoint — must upload videos first then assemble by reference

unported.item: autoScrollInterval listed as an importable/uploadable metadata field — absent from the new public OpenAPI spec (not in ImportItem or UploadRequest schemas) and not in any Fumadocs page; appears to be a dropped/phantom field rather than an intentional rename

notes: Fully ported across import-content + tiktok/instagram/status generated pages plus upload-videos, webhooks, and carousels guides. Only gap: phantom autoScrollInterval field, dropped from spec.

fumadocsTargets.covers: The full GET /v1/content endpoint: query params (limit default 20 max 100, cursor, uploadStatus, tags any-match) plus publishStatus/status, and request/response examples — all OpenAPI-generated. Description text covers the Pagination and Filtering sections.
Cursor-based Pagination narrative: nextCursor in meta, passing cursor for next page (example uses /v1/content), absent nextCursor = last page, opaque-token caveat.

notes: All params, examples, pagination and filtering content represented in generated list-content page + api/index pagination section. 'Filtering coming soon' note dropped intentionally (publishStatus now a real param).

OpenAPI-generated response example carrying the wire content object: id, contentType, title, description, uploadStatus, publishStatus, duration, streamingUrl, thumbnailUrl, section, author, tags, customMetadata, captionTracks (full sub-fields), images, publishAt, expiresAt, videoWidth/Height, aspectRatio, maxFrameRate, videoQuality, resolutionTier, playbackId, liveStreamId, createdAt, updatedAt
Creating content with contentType video/carousel/video_carousel; carousels created ready; videos start waiting_for_upload; publishAt/expiresAt scheduling params

Creating content via upload/import; upload (uploadStatus) lifecycle table (waiting_for_upload/processing/ready/errored — renamed from pending/error); carousels skip processing; language auto-detection note

Image vs video carousel creation; carousels ready immediately; empty video carousel excluded from feed; autoScrollInterval context; video-carousel management endpoints (add/reorder/remove videos, max 10); deletion/archiving

Publish lifecycle (published/unpublished), auto-publish, scheduling (publishAt/expiresAt), the four feed-eligibility conditions, live-stream auto publish/unpublish, deletion archives and removes from feed
customMetadata.language BCP 47 detail, asynchronous after ready, 'und' value semantics
POST /v1/content/{id}/videos — add video references to carousel
PUT /v1/content/{id}/videos/order — reorder carousel videos
DELETE /v1/content/{id}/videos/{entry_id} — remove video from carousel
DELETE /v1/content/{id} archives content (generated endpoint page)

unported.item: Enum value lists/descriptions for resolutionTier (audio-only/SD/HD/FHD/UHD) and videoQuality (basic/plus) appear only as the example values 'HD'/'plus' in the generated get-content example; the full enumerated set and per-tier meaning are not documented in prose or a typed schema.
Field length caveats (title max 200, description max 2000, section/author max 200 chars) from the object's attribute table are not surfaced in the new prose pages.

notes: Object page is OpenAPI-generated by design; all fields/lifecycles/behaviors live in content-model + feeds + upload/carousel guides + generated endpoint examples. Only minor enum/char-limit prose details unported.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire page: GET /v1/content/search endpoint, description (title/description/tags/section/author search), query params q (required string, min 1/max 500) and limit (integer default 20, min 1/max 50), request examples, and 200 response with data array fields + meta.request_id — all auto-generated from openapi.public.json which matches the Mintlify params and response example exactly

notes: Fully ported to generated OpenAPI page api/content/search-content; q/limit params and response fields verified against openapi.public.json.

fumadocsTargets.covers: The PATCH /v1/content/{content_id} endpoint reference (OpenAPI-generated). Frontmatter description carries merge-by-key/null-removes-key semantics, publishStatus=published requires processing finished + clears publishAt, and the 400 validation rules (past publishAt/expiresAt, expiresAt must be after publishAt). 200 response example shows the full content object (id, contentType, title, description, uploadStatus, publishStatus, duration, tags, customMetadata, section, author, publishAt, expiresAt, streamingUrl, thumbnailUrl, dimensions, timestamps). Note: spec has no requestBody, so the request body param table is NOT auto-generated on this page — it lives in the captions-and-metadata guide instead.

Hand-written PATCH content workflow: path param usage, request body param table (title, description, tags, section, author, publishStatus, publishAt, expiresAt, customMetadata, origin) with replace-vs-merge notes; cURL/Python/Node.js request examples; full Merge semantics section (add/overwrite/null-removes/omit-untouched) with before/after example; Scheduling-and-publishing-interact callout (publishStatus=published clears publishAt); Retroactive thumbnail re-extraction section for origin=ios_upload on already-processed content (color-accurate thumbnails, fires only on value change, same-value no-op).

Field semantics: customMetadata as a string->JSONValue map (matches the object/null merge model), autoScrollInterval (carousel auto-scroll, seconds), and the content object fields surfaced in the response (title, description, duration, tags, author, section, captionTracks, images, streamingUrl, thumbnailUrl, etc.).
Cross-referenced origin=ios_upload rationale (why iOS-recorded content needs the flag for color-accurate thumbnails); mirrors the Mintlify page's 'See Upload -> origin' link.

unported.item: autoScrollInterval listed as a PATCH-updatable request body field (carousel auto-scroll interval in seconds). It appears in content-model only as a read-side feed field, and is absent from the OpenAPI spec and from the captions-and-metadata/carousels PATCH param tables — so the ability to update a carousel's auto-scroll interval via PATCH is not documented as a settable field.

notes: Endpoint ref is OpenAPI-generated (no requestBody in spec); param table + merge/origin behavior fully ported to captions-and-metadata guide. Only gap: autoScrollInterval as a settable PATCH field.

fumadocsTargets.covers: The POST /v1/content/upload endpoint itself: request body params (contentType, title, description, tags, section, author, customMetadata, origin, publishAt, expiresAt, images w/ position+altText via UploadImageSlot, callbackUrl) auto-generated from schema, plus video and carousel response examples with signed uploadUrl/imageSlots. Publishable+secret key note in description.

Uploading a video narrative (create session -> PUT file -> poll), pending/processing/ready status lifecycle, origin/ios_upload thumbnail note, callbackUrl push-vs-poll, bulk-upload-in-parallel (no batch endpoint; import for bulk), automatic captions/language detection mention.

Uploading a carousel (image slots), creating a video_carousel and adding existing ready videos by reference (up to 10, reorder), empty video carousels excluded from feed. Replaces the Mintlify inline-slot/video_carousel-via-upload flow with the new IA (POST /v1/content + image/video endpoints).

Callback events section in full: content.imported (processing/ready) video, content.import_failed (error), content.imported carousel (ready/errored, images array), playbackId-may-be-null note, externalId reflects provided value, retry behavior (3 attempts, 1s then 5s, abandon), fire-and-forget, missed-event recovery via GET /v1/content/{id}.
The Note about automatic caption generation and BCP 47 language detection stored as customMetadata.language.
autoScrollInterval carousel field semantics (seconds between automatic page transitions), which is absent from the upload request schema.

notes: All substantive content ported. Carousel/video_carousel via /v1/content/upload + inline images[]/autoScrollInterval restructured to new IA (carousels guide + content-model); webhooks fully covered.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire page ported near-verbatim: error response envelope JSON example, code/message error object fields (ResponseField converted to a table), HTTP status code table (400/401/403/404/409/429/500), error-handling Python example, rate limiting (120 req/min sliding window) with 429 example, Tip->Callout for higher limits, and retry strategy (exponential backoff + 500 handling).

notes: Dedicated Fumadocs api/errors.mdx is a near-verbatim port; all sections, tables, code examples and caveats present (only ResponseField->table and Tip->Callout formatting changes).

fumadocsTargets.covers: The POST /v1/live-streams/{stream_id}/clips endpoint: path param (stream_id), body params (clips array maxItems 20 with startSec/endSec required + optional title, callbackUrl), 201 response example with contentId/playbackId/startSec/endSec/title and meta.request_id, generated request/response schema (replaces hand-written param tables and curl/python/node examples).

Clipping narrative: real-time clipping while active or after ended, hls-only (409 for webrtc), processing->ready availability, clips created unpublished + PATCH to publish, callbackUrl POST on ready, secret-key requirement and the 'clip this moment' server-forward pattern with rate limits/attribution/abuse controls (Letting end users create clips section), and the 409 reason (recording not available yet / unsupported state).

Generic status-code semantics referenced by the endpoint: 400 invalid_request (malformed/invalid param values, e.g. endSec not > startSec, >20 clips), 404 not_found (stream missing or not in your org), 409 conflict.

notes: Fully ported: create-clips generated page (schema/example) + live-streams guide (behavior, warning, end-user pattern, 409) + errors page (400/404/409). 422 in spec subsumes hand-written 400.

fumadocsTargets.covers: OpenAPI-generated endpoint page for POST /v1/live-streams: request body params (title, protocol, latencyMode default low, reconnectWindow default 60 range 0-1800 + extra reconnectSlateUrl/customMetadata), 201 response example with all data fields (id, playbackId, status idle, streamingProtocol, title, latencyMode, reconnectWindow, rtmpUrl, streamKey, timestamps), and request code samples

All prose/behaviors: idle->active auto-transition and feed visibility; choosing-a-protocol section (hls default few-seconds vs webrtc sub-second, encoder still uses rtmpUrl); latencyMode/reconnectWindow apply to hls only and ignored for webrtc; #latency-mode section (low/reduced/standard semantics, target of the Mintlify object#latency-mode link); single-use streamKey + rtmpUrl returned only on create / not retrievable / delete+recreate if lost; credential-secrets warning callout

notes: Fully ported: params+response via generated create-live-stream page, all behaviors/caveats/warning in api/guides/live-streams. 'protocol' renamed to 'streamingProtocol' (intentional schema change).

fumadocsTargets.covers: DELETE /v1/live-streams/{stream_id} endpoint: description (delete + remove from feed, ends active broadcast first, clips preserved), path param stream_id (string, required), secret-key auth, 200 response example with id + deleted:true + meta.request_id — all generated from openapi.public.json
Global 404 not_found behavior: 'requested resource doesn't exist, or doesn't belong to your organization' — covers the Mintlify page's 404 note

notes: Fully ported. Endpoint is OpenAPI-generated (path renamed {id}->{stream_id}); all params, description, behaviors, response example present. 404 note covered by api/errors.

fumadocsTargets.covers: The full endpoint: POST /v1/live-streams/{stream_id}/end. OpenAPI-generated page covering the path parameter (stream_id, required UUID), the description (end regardless of broadcaster connection, async status->ended transition, poll status/webhook to confirm, recording becomes available for clipping), and the 200 response schema/example with all fields (id, playbackId, status, title, latencyMode, reconnectWindow, startedAt, endedAt, createdAt).

Prose narrative of ending a stream: the 'Ending a stream' section (use case, immediate close vs reconnectWindow auto-end), what 'ended' means per protocol, recording/clipping availability, and the restart/permanence caveat (refined: hls can reactivate, webrtc is permanent).
Generic 404 not-found behavior for streams that don't exist or don't belong to your organization.

notes: Fully ported. OpenAPI-generated endpoint page covers param + response; live-streams guide covers end behavior/permanence. Mintlify 'permanent' Note intentionally refined (hls can reactivate).

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire page: GET /v1/live-streams/{stream_id} (mapped from {id}). OpenAPI-generated page covers the path param (stream_id, UUID), secret-key auth, status/startedAt/endedAt/createdAt/playbackId/title/latencyMode/reconnectWindow response fields (in the generated 200 example), the 404-if-not-found-or-not-your-org behavior (in the page description), and auto-generated cURL/Python/Node request examples.

unported.item: Mintlify caveat 'rtmpUrl and streamKey are NOT returned by this endpoint; only on the Create Live Stream response.' The new generated GET page's 200 example INCLUDES both rtmpUrl and streamKey, and its description says it returns 'ingest credentials' — the API behavior was intentionally changed to return these on GET, so the old caveat is now factually inverted rather than a missing piece of info.

notes: Maps 1:1 to generated api/live-streams/get-live-stream; all fields/params/behaviors present. Old 'no rtmpUrl/streamKey on GET' caveat is now inverted by an intentional API change.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire page: GET /v1/live-streams endpoint, description (reverse-chronological list, secret-key auth), query params limit (1-100, default 20) and status (idle/active/ended), response fields (id, playbackId, status, title, latencyMode, reconnectWindow, startedAt, endedAt, createdAt), and request/response examples — all OpenAPI-generated

unported.item: Mintlify caveat that the list response does NOT include rtmpUrl or streamKey (credentials only on Create). The new generated schema reverses this — list entries now include streamKey/rtmpUrl/whipUrl/whipToken and the description says 'Each entry includes ingest credentials.' Appears to be an intentional API/schema change, so the old caveat has no home and is contradicted by design.

notes: All endpoint info (params, fields, examples) covered by OpenAPI-generated api/live-streams/list-live-streams. Old 'no credentials in list' caveat intentionally reversed by new schema.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Object fields via the generated POST 201 response example: id, playbackId, status, title, streamingProtocol (=protocol), latencyMode, reconnectWindow, rtmpUrl, streamKey, startedAt, endedAt, createdAt, currentViewers. Request schema captures protocol/latencyMode enums and reconnectWindow 0-1800 default 60.
Full object shape via generated GET 200 example including status=active and currentViewers populated; confirms startedAt/endedAt semantics.

Lifecycle (idle/active/ended table + transition rules), Latency mode table (low/reduced/standard), rtmpUrl/streamKey secret-handling warning, reconnectWindow auto-end behavior, recording-to-VOD, reconnect slate, clipping from recordings, ending behavior per protocol.
'Live streams in the feed' section: isLive=true flag, currentViewers concurrent viewer count, liveStreamStatus, ended-state behavior (isLive flips to false, currentViewers returns 0).
Live streams surface as ContentItem with contentType/isLive; live_stream feed-item semantics, liveStreamId, liveStreamStatus including dvr (recording-as-on-demand-video).
Deletion section: ends the broadcast if active, removes/archives the content item, clips preserved as standalone content items.

unported.item: Title 'Max 200 characters' constraint: present in the Mintlify object field doc but the generated CreateLiveStreamRequest schema has no maxLength on title (title is otherwise documented in examples and guide).

notes: Object page fully ported: fields live in generated create/get examples; lifecycle/latency/feed-fields/deletion all in guides + content-model. Only a trivial title maxLength constraint is absent.

fumadocsTargets.covers: The generated endpoint page for GET /v1/live-streams/status: purpose, publishable-key auth (safe from client), playback_ids query param (up to 50, comma-separated), request/response examples auto-generated from OpenAPI, response shape (status/startedAt/endedAt + added currentViewers), silent omission of unowned IDs / empty data behavior, and when-to-use-vs-feed guidance (feed items already include liveStreamStatus).

Status lifecycle semantics: the status enum idle/active/ended and their meanings (lifecycle table), the client polling pattern via GET /v1/live-streams/status?playback_ids=... with publishable key (line 33), and feed items carrying liveStreamStatus for initial render.
401 error for missing/invalid publishable key; publishable-key auth model that makes this endpoint safe to call directly from client apps.
Error response semantics: 400 invalid_request (malformed request / bad parameter values, e.g. too many playback IDs) and 401 invalid_api_key.
The 'inspect full stream configuration with your secret key' alternative the Mintlify page links to (Get Live Stream), in lieu of the removed standalone Live Stream Object page.

notes: All info ported. Endpoint is OpenAPI-generated (status/startedAt/endedAt in response example; 400/401 via generic errors+auth pages). Mintlify's 400/401 table surfaces as 422 in spec + generic error pages; /v1/feed link intentionally dropped. currentViewers added.

notes: api/overview.mdx is a 1:1 port to Fumadocs api/index (every section identical); auth and error references resolve to api/authentication and api/errors. Complete.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Full endpoint: POST /v1/surveys. Request body params (title, question, status, priority, autoAdvanceDelay, options[text,position], placementRules[ruleType,ruleValue]) auto-generated from OpenAPI schema with maxLength/defaults/required. 201 response example with all fields (id, status, options[id,text,position,responseCount,createdAt], placementRules[id,ruleType,ruleValue,createdAt], totalResponses, createdAt, updatedAt, meta.request_id). Rich description covers status enum behaviors (draft/active/paused/archived), all rule types (min_videos_before, once_per_user, once_per_session, max_per_session), the required min_videos_before non-negative-integer-n validation rule, and priority tie-breaking.
Generic 400 invalid_request behavior covering missing required fields and invalid parameter values — conceptually the validation-error conditions from the Mintlify error table.

unported.item: Exact validation error message strings and the condition->message->status(400) mapping table (e.g. 'placement_rules must include a min_videos_before rule', 'min_videos_before rule_value.n must be a non-negative integer', 'Invalid rule_type: {value}', 'Invalid status: {value}'). The validation rules themselves are described and 400 is covered generically, but the literal copy-paste messages and the specific 400 code are not reproduced (OpenAPI shows generic 422).

notes: Generated OpenAPI page covers all params, response fields, and examples; rich description ports status/rule-type behaviors and min_videos_before validation. Only literal error-message strings missing (low).

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire page: DELETE /v1/surveys/{id} endpoint — path param (survey_id, required), archive behavior (status=archived, removed from feed, response data preserved, still retrievable), and 200 response example ({id, deleted:true, meta.request_id}). OpenAPI-generated from openapi.public.json operation delete /v1/surveys/{survey_id}.

notes: Fully ported. OpenAPI-generated delete-survey page carries the path param, archive semantics, and response example; request code samples auto-generated.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire endpoint: GET /v1/surveys/{id} (survey_id path param), description, bearer auth, and full 200 response example with all fields (id, title, question, status, priority, autoAdvanceDelay, options[id/text/position/responseCount/createdAt], placementRules[id/ruleType/ruleValue/createdAt], totalResponses, createdAt, updatedAt, meta.request_id). Request examples auto-generated; param table auto-generated from schema.
The 404 'does not exist or does not belong to your organization' caveat, documented globally (line 39: 404 not_found = resource doesn't exist or doesn't belong to your organization).

notes: Fully ported. OpenAPI-generated get-survey page carries identical 200 example and all object fields; 404 org-scoping caveat lives in api/errors.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire page: GET /v1/surveys endpoint, status query param (draft/active/paused/archived), priority-descending ordering note, per-option responseCount and totalResponses, full survey object response schema/example (id, title, question, status, priority, autoAdvanceDelay, options, placementRules, totalResponses, timestamps, meta.request_id) — OpenAPI-generated

notes: Fully ported. OpenAPI-generated list-surveys page carries the status param, ordering note, and complete survey object schema/example matching the Mintlify page.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Full survey object structure and all fields — id, title, question, status, priority, autoAdvanceDelay, options (id/text/position/responseCount/createdAt), placementRules (id/ruleType/ruleValue/createdAt), totalResponses, createdAt, updatedAt — appear in the OpenAPI-generated 200 response example.

Status lifecycle semantics (draft is composing state, only active injected, paused removes from feed preserving data, archived reachable only via delete); full placement-rules catalog (min_videos_before required, once_per_user, once_per_session, max_per_session with ruleValue shapes/behaviors); X-User-Id/X-Session-Id header evaluation and skip behavior; highest-priority-wins tie-break. Also field maxLengths (option text 200, question 500) via request schema.
Status-transition mechanism (update the status field between non-archived states); options/placementRules replacement semantics.
Archived status reached only by DELETE; sets status to archived, removes from feed, preserves response data.
Surveys ordered by priority (highest first); status filter (draft/active/paused/archived); includes options, placement rules, response counts.
Priority as the tie-breaker for placement when multiple active surveys are eligible for the same slot.

notes: All object fields, status lifecycle, and placement-rule semantics from the Mintlify object page are ported into the generated survey endpoint pages (response example + descriptions/schemas). Standalone object page absence is by design.

fumadocsTargets.covers: The endpoint itself: path param survey_id, request body (optionId/userId required, sessionId/deviceInfo optional), 201 response example with all fields (id, surveyId, optionId, userId, respondedAt, meta.request_id) — auto-generated from OpenAPI. Description frontmatter restates the publishable-key/SDK behavior plus the 409 duplicate and 400 non-active error rules.

Publishable-key (X-API-Key, pk_live_) auth model, 'called by the SDK automatically' behavior, and an explicit table row listing POST /v1/surveys/{id}/responses as a directly-callable publishable-key endpoint.

Error conditions: 400 invalid_request (survey not active / option not in survey), 404 not_found (survey not found / wrong org), 409 conflict (explicitly noted as a duplicate survey response). Plus 403 forbidden for wrong key type.

unported.item: The granular error-row distinction 'Option not in survey' (400) is not restated as its own labeled condition; it is only covered generically by the 400 invalid_request row in api/errors.

notes: Fully ported. Endpoint params/response are OpenAPI-generated in submit-survey-response; auth and error semantics covered in authentication.mdx and errors.mdx.

fumadocsTargets.covers: Entire endpoint: PUT /v1/surveys/{survey_id}; path param survey_id; all 7 optional body fields (title max200, question max500, status, priority, autoAdvanceDelay, options[text/position], placementRules[ruleType/ruleValue]); replace-not-merge semantics (in description); request + response examples auto-generated from schema; 200 response object fields (id,title,question,status,priority,autoAdvanceDelay,options[id/text/position/responseCount/createdAt],placementRules[id/ruleType/ruleValue/createdAt],totalResponses,createdAt,updatedAt,meta.request_id)

fumadocsTargets.covers: Video upload workflow: create upload session (POST /v1/content/upload with title/description/tags/customMetadata), PUT file to signed URL, status lifecycle (waiting_for_upload/processing/ready/errored), poll GET /v1/content/{id} for uploadStatus, playbackId+duration in ready response, feed eligibility, auto caption + language detection (customMetadata.language BCP-47), iOS origin:ios_upload flag, callbackUrl push alternative

Post-creation management: add captions (POST .../captions, file/language/label, immediately ready), update metadata (PATCH /v1/content/{id}, customMetadata shallow-merge semantics), publication URLs (POST/PUT/order/DELETE, position 0 -> publisherUrl), retroactive origin:ios_upload re-extraction, archive via DELETE

Carousel data-model fields autoScrollInterval and caption (as SDK-facing FeedItem fields), captionTracks, customMetadata semantics
